title: Counterfactual stop-now value features from strategy optimization
summary: Pit-stop literature frames the choice as a compare-now-vs-wait optimization under degradation, pit loss, and remaining distance.
rationale: Pit-stop literature frames the choice as a compare-now-vs-wait optimization under degradation, pit loss, and remaining distance. That suggests more useful features than raw wear ratios: approximate marginal benefit of stopping now, optimal stint-length gap, and undercut feasibility.
implementation_hint: Estimate per-compound degradation slope from historical laps, then compute features such as current stint length minus approximate optimal stint length sqrt(2P/d), expected lap-time loss from one more lap, stop-now finishability on fresh compound, and a simple undercut margin using current vs fresh-tyre pace assumptions.
expected_effect: Can turn existing heuristics into more causal, race-time-based signals and improve edge cases where identical TyreLife values imply different actions because laps remaining differ.
risk: Needs assumptions for pit-loss and fresh-tyre pace; if those are too crude, the features may just repackage noise.
